title: Ability to reorder automations recipes
excerpt: I’d like to be able to move the automations around to view in a sequential order. It will make it much easier to confirm my processes are correct. I use a number now and then take a screen shot and align in PPT, which is…

posts.author: abossinas (Alison Bossinas)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d like to be able to move the automations around to view in a sequential order. It will make it much easier to confirm my processes are correct. I use a number now and then take a screen shot and align in PPT, which is very time consuming. I have quite a few boards that have more than 20 automations. A new board I’m working on will have upwards of 50.

image1053×765 103 KB

posts.author: zach-xpndinteractive (Zachary)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a bunch of automatons that all happen at the same time. Would be nice to be able to reorder them so that they came out properly. Right now they are in the opposite order because I copied them from top to bottom.

Again, just a drag-drop to reorder the automations to organize them properly.

posts.author: zach1 (Zach)
posts.cookedHtml:
Fellow Zach here - I completely agree, some sort of organization of automations would be much appreciated.

The good news is you can at least search these with Control + F + F (or command on Mac).

posts.author: zach-xpndinteractive (Zachary)
posts.cookedHtml: That’s not even a monday.com feature you can search anything with control+f

posts.author: zach-xpndinteractive (Zachary)
posts.cookedHtml: Cant even set like 7:01am 7:02am to fix it I literally have to remake all the automatons. Pretty ridiculous that this isn’t already a feature.

posts.author: dwonhgi (David Won)
posts.cookedHtml: Automations are a key feature and highlight for the monday.com platform. However, it’s limited by the subscription level. If this is as important as monday.com says it is, then either 1) the limits should apply to the number of automation recipes…not the actions or 2) significantly increase the automation action limits per subscription level to allow this benefit to workflow execution a true feature of the platform. In my case, I have many items that need to progress through a workflow which is made significantly easier through the application of automation. Many items have up to 30 actions each.

posts.author: mondaydoughnutsartic (Scott Keller)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be great if we could simply re-order and organize automations on a board to make more logical sense as a flow. I don’t mean the ability to have automation 1 happen before automation 2, I simply mean being able to drag and re-order them so they appear in a logical flow, especially when we get 10+ automations, it can be confusing to have to search for and remember where certain automations reside. Being able to re-organize them into a logical flow would help with this.

posts.author: Roberta (Roberta)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be very helpful to be able to drag and drop to reorder the order of things being entered into an automation.
For example: When Item (Publish article) is created, create subitem (write first draft) and create subitem (write second draft) and create subitem (send first draft for review).
These are out of order and there’s no way to drag and drop to correct it. I have to delete the things and reenter them in the correct order.

posts.author: becca_colesoft (Becca C)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same issue. It is extremely frustrating to realize I need to change a step and not be able to insert or reorder the existing steps! Or duplicate a step within the same recipe.

posts.author: AutumnImbro11 (Autumn Imbro)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same issue. Please consider making this improvement.

posts.author: CKJ (Curtis Johnston)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to be able to drag automations in the list, to re-order them.

Just like you can drag items to re-order them on boards.

It seems like a simple improvement to allow this.

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Curtis! Shannon here with the monday team. I’ve merged your feature request with his existing one to ensure that votes for this particular request are accurately counted!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Roberta! Thank you for submitting your feature request. I’ve merged your feature request to this existing one in order to accurately count votes! Your request had 5 votes, which have now been added to this request here.

posts.author: Roberta (Roberta)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on whether this is happening and when? I just ran into another scenario where the ability to reorder the items in an automation would have been very, very helpful

posts.author: AshcroftJewellery (Christian Fox )
posts.cookedHtml:
Unfortunately the original request was from 2020 (merged with a 2023 thread :D)…

This is becoming more frustrating, as I also have run into this problem a few times recently. The amount of work required to continuously monitor/update automations is a bit of a headache! I have a lot of concerns about the development/communication of this platform at the moment.
